tool.description:
Perform literal or regular-expression replacements on a text file. Line endings are normalized internally, so patterns/replacements that include newlines are supported.

Important (read carefully):
- Always run with preview=true first to inspect hit locations and the diff preview.
- Do NOT include raw newline characters in pattern/replacement. Use the two-character sequence \n instead (JSON: \\n). Raw newlines can corrupt source files (e.g., Python string literals).
- When mode=regex, pattern is interpreted as a Python re pattern (not a plain substring). For example, \x is invalid; write \xNN (e.g., \x00, \x1b).
- If you only need to match a backslash literally, prefer mode=literal.
system_prompttool.system_promptThis tool performs literal or regex replacements on a text file.

Recommended workflow:
1) Inspect the target area with read_file
2) Run replace_in_file with preview=true and verify hit locations + diff
3) If correct, apply with preview=false (a .org/.orgN backup will be created)
4) If you edited a .py file, run python -m py_compile for a syntax check

Newlines (most important):
- Do not include raw newlines in JSON strings.
  - OK: aaa\nbbb (JSON: aaa\\nbbb)
  - NG: aaa<newline>bbb (can break source files and cause SyntaxError)

Regex notes:
- pattern is a Python re pattern
- \x is invalid (re.error); use \xNN (e.g., \x00)
- Use mode=literal if you only need plain substring matching
- In replacement, \1, \2, ... refer to capture groups; referencing a non-existent group is an error
- In replacement, \n means a backslash + n, not an actual newline

Windows path note (especially when editing .py):
- In Python string literals, backslashes must be escaped (e.g., C:\\path).

Safety:
- When preview=false and the operation is risky (dangerous path, many matches, etc.), the tool may require confirmation or cancel automatically.parameterstypeobjectpropertiespathtypestringdescriptionparam.path.descriptionTarget file path (recommended: under workdir).modetypestringenumliteralregexdescriptionparam.mode.descriptionReplacement mode: literal (plain) or regex (Python re).defaultliteralpatterntypestringdescriptionparam.pattern.descriptionSearch pattern. To express a newline, write \n (JSON: \\n).replacementtypestringdescriptionparam.replacement.descriptionReplacement text.counttypeintegernulldescriptionparam.count.descriptionMaximum number of replacements. If null, replace all matches.defaultpreviewtypebooleandescriptionparam.preview.descriptionIf true, return a preview only and do not modify the file.defaultcontext_linestypeintegerdescriptionparam.context_lines.descriptionNumber of context lines to include before/after each hit in the preview.defaultconfirm_if_matches_overtypeintegerdescriptionparam.confirm_if_matches_over.descriptionWhen preview=false, require confirmation if the number of matches is >= this value.defaultencodingtypestringdescriptionparam.encoding.descriptionFile encoding (default: utf-8).defaultutf-8raw_newline_policytypestringenumallowrejectdescriptionparam.raw_newline_policy.descriptionHow to handle raw newline characters (\n/\r) in pattern/replacement. allow=permit, reject=cancel automatically (no confirmation).defaultallowrequiredpathpatternreplacementRead a text file and return (content, detected_newlines, encoding_used).file too large: {size} > {max_bytes} bytesrstrictutf-8replaceReturn unified diff string ( if no changes).a/{path}b/{path}Write text back using the original newline convention.
